# Stellar Parallax

Stellar parallax is the apparent shift in a nearby star's position when viewed from
opposite sides of Earth's orbit around the Sun. By measuring this tiny angle and
knowing the diameter of Earth's orbit, astronomers calculate the star's distance
through simple trigonometry. It was the first direct method to measure distances to
the stars.

## Why the angles are tiny

Even the nearest stars are so far away that their parallax angles are a fraction of
an arcsecond, far too small to see by eye. The unit called the parsec is defined as
the distance at which a star shows a parallax of one arcsecond. Space telescopes
like Gaia measure parallax for over a billion stars, mapping the structure of our
galaxy with great precision.
